raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What is the primary goal for this section review: (A) strengthening the core scientific argument (logic, evidence-claim alignment, novelty) or (B) improving the writing and narrative flow (polish, clarity, structure)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The review will assess if the central contribution claim is framed clearly and effectively against the background motivation.",
    "R1.2": "The analysis will verify the logical soundness of the argument connecting the causal methodology to the final practitioner recommendation.",
    "R1.3": "The review will confirm that the conclusions drawn, such as the effect of heterogeneity, are directly supported by the numerical results reported in the text.",
    "R1.4": "The feedback will evaluate the strength and defensibility of the multiple 'first to' claims made regarding the causal and IV approaches.",
    "R1.6": "The review will include sentence-level suggestions to improve grammar, conciseness, and overall readability.",
    "R1.7": "The feedback will consider whether the claims of novelty are well-supported by a thorough engagement with existing literature on multi-agent LLMs.",
    "RX.1": "The review will ensure that all quantitative claims are accompanied by appropriate statements of statistical uncertainty, such as the provided confidence interval.",
    "RX.2": "The analysis will scrutinize strong recommendations like 'dominant design choice' to ensure they are not overstated relative to the supporting evidence.",
    "RX.3": "The feedback provided will be strictly limited to the content and claims presented within the provided artifact section.",
    "RX.4": "The review will consider whether the practitioner recommendations could inadvertently lead to negative outcomes, such as premature optimization on a narrow set of models.",
    "RX.5": "The analysis will check that the origins of the experimental data and methodological choices are clearly explained and traceable."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What is the primary goal for this section review: (A) strengthening the core scientific argument (logic, evidence-claim alignment, novelty) or (B) improving the writing and narrative flow (polish, clarity, structure)?

rationale.R1.1: The review will assess if the central contribution claim is framed clearly and effectively against the background motivation.
rationale.R1.2: The analysis will verify the logical soundness of the argument connecting the causal methodology to the final practitioner recommendation.
rationale.R1.3: The review will confirm that the conclusions drawn, such as the effect of heterogeneity, are directly supported by the numerical results reported in the text.
rationale.R1.4: The feedback will evaluate the strength and defensibility of the multiple 'first to' claims made regarding the causal and IV approaches.
rationale.R1.6: The review will include sentence-level suggestions to improve grammar, conciseness, and overall readability.
rationale.R1.7: The feedback will consider whether the claims of novelty are well-supported by a thorough engagement with existing literature on multi-agent LLMs.
rationale.RX.1: The review will ensure that all quantitative claims are accompanied by appropriate statements of statistical uncertainty, such as the provided confidence interval.
rationale.RX.2: The analysis will scrutinize strong recommendations like 'dominant design choice' to ensure they are not overstated relative to the supporting evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The feedback provided will be strictly limited to the content and claims presented within the provided artifact section.
rationale.RX.4: The review will consider whether the practitioner recommendations could inadvertently lead to negative outcomes, such as premature optimization on a narrow set of models.
rationale.RX.5: The analysis will check that the origins of the experimental data and methodological choices are clearly explained and traceable.
